name: Peñalta Summit Trail from Benatae
name_original: Cumbre del Peñalta desde Benatae - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: Hike the moderate 7.0-mile (11.2 km) Cumbre del Peñalta circular route from Benatae for 360-degree panoramic views of the Sierra de Segura.
region_intro: Hiking around Benatae offers access to the Sierra de Segura, a region characterized by mountainous terrain, extensive pine forests, and numerous natural springs. A significant portion of the area lies within the Cazorla Segura and Las Villas Natural Park, known for its valleys, limestone quarries, and high plateaus. The landscape includes prominent peaks like Mount Peñalta and El Púlpito, providing panoramic views. This natural setting supports a diverse ecosystem and provides varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Benatae The most popular hiking route is Peña del Olivar Loop Trail, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete, offering views of the surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castle of Segura de la Sierra from Orcera - N. P. of the Sierras of Cazorla, Segura and las Villas, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.6 km) path, traversing varied terrain within the natural park and leading towards historical landmarks. Local hikers also love the Peñalta Summit Trail from Benatae, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through pine forests towards the summit of Mount Peñalta, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Benatae is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and abundant springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging summit ascents. The routes in Benatae are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Benatae's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Benatae?
A: Benatae offers a wide range of hiking opportunities, with over 30 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents within the Sierra de Segura.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Benatae?
A: The terrain around Benatae is predominantly mountainous, characterized by extensive pine forests and numerous natural springs. A significant portion of the area lies within the Cazorla Segura and Las Villas Natural Park, featuring valleys, limestone quarries, and high plateaus. You'll encounter varied paths, from forest tracks to rocky ascents, offering a diverse hiking experience.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, there are challenging routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983578848/puntal-de-la-misa-to-las-gorgollitas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Misa to Las Gorgollitas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as difficult and covers approximately 11.2 km with significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding adventure.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Benatae is rich in natural beauty and offers stunning viewpoints. The region is known for its abundant springs, such as Atajadero and San Miguel, with the Área Recreativa Fuente San Miguel being a popular starting point for routes. Peaks like Mount Peñalta and El Púlpito provide fantastic panoramic views. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2556115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalta de Benatae (1,412 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Misa (1,796 m)&lt;/a&gt; offering impressive vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions accessible by hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region combines natural beauty with historical interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984764430/castle-of-segura-de-la-sierra-from-orcera-n-p-of-the-sierras-of-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra from Orcera&lt;/a&gt; route leads towards historical landmarks, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5239771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; itself, offering a blend of hiking and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benatae?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available for those who prefer to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984916956/pena-del-olivar-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Olivar Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.7 km path that offers scenic views of the surrounding natural park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Benatae?
A: The trails in Benatae are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de Segura, and the diverse landscapes encountered on the routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Benatae?
A: While specific 'easy' family routes are not detailed, the area offers a range of difficulties. With 5 easy routes among the total, families can find suitable paths for shorter, less strenuous walks, especially around recreational areas like Fuente San Miguel, which provide a pleasant environment for all ages.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs in Benatae?
A: The Sierra de Segura, being a natural park, generally welcomes dogs on trails, especially if kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. It's always advisable to check specific park regulations or signage at trailheads, but the natural environment is conducive to hiking with canine companions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benatae?
A: Benatae is an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The spring and autumn months typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with lush landscapes in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings cooler temperatures and the possibility of snow at higher altitudes, offering a different kind of beauty.

Q: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or shelters along the trails?
A: Yes, the area provides some facilities to enhance your hiking experience. You can find a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4769649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charming picnic area&lt;/a&gt; and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5524225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Era del Fustal Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. There are also &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drinking water sources&lt;/a&gt; available, which are essential for longer hikes.

Q: How can I reach the trailheads, and is parking available?
A: Benatae is nestled within the Sierra de Segura, and many trailheads are accessible from or near the village center. The Área Recreativa Fuente San Miguel is a known starting point for several routes. While specific parking details for every trailhead are not listed, rural areas like Benatae typically offer parking options near popular access points or village outskirts. Public transport options might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to access the more remote trails.
tips:
Summit located in the municipality of Benatae in the region of La Sierra de Segura.

The Peñalta peak is 1,412 meters high and is located between the towns of Orcera and Benata.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Cumbre del Peñalta trail?
faqs.answer: The Cumbre del Peñalta trail is rated as having medium difficulty. It requires good physical condition and steady footing, especially on the ascending zigzag paths and along the airy crest. While not technically difficult for climbing, some parts are steep and challenging.

faqs.question: Where does the Cumbre del Peñalta circular trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Fuente de San Miguel recreational area in Benatae. You should find parking available in or near this area, which serves as a common starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Cumbre del Peñalta trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain. You'll encounter rapidly ascending paths, forest tracks, and smaller trails winding through beautiful, shaded forests of oaks and pines. The ascent to the summit involves marked paths with several zigzags, leading to an airy crest. While generally well-trodden, some sections, particularly on the descent, may be less defined.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the summit of Peñalta de Benatae (1,412 m). From here, you'll enjoy spectacular 360-degree panoramic views of the entire Sierra de Segura and a significant portion of the Natural Park of Cazorla, Segura y las Villas. You can spot various towns like Siles, La Puerta de Segura, and Segura de la Sierra. The summit also has tables and benches for enjoying the views.

faqs.question: Is the Cumbre del Peñalta trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Natural Park of Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers, especially given the trail's moderate difficulty and some less defined sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Cumbre del Peñalta?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow or icy conditions at higher elevations, making the trail more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Natural Park of Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or specific hiking permits required for general access to trails like Cumbre del Peñalta within the Natural Park of Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially if planning activities like camping or specific guided tours, as these might have different rules.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other paths. You will traverse parts of the 'Fuente de San Miguel - Cumbre de Peñalta' and 'El Pilarillo - Peñalta' trails, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Cumbre del Peñalta trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its medium difficulty, distance of over 11 km, and significant elevation gain, this trail is best suited for families with older children who are experienced hikers and have good physical stamina. The airy crest, though secured with railings, might also be a consideration for younger children.

faqs.question: What makes the Cumbre del Peñalta trail notable?
faqs.answer: This trail is notable for its spectacular 360-degree panoramic views from the summit, its strategic location as the highest point in Benatae, and the safety measures like railings along the 'airy' crest. It offers a prime example of the diverse hiking opportunities within the vast Natural Park of Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Benatae?
faqs.answer: Benatae is a small town, and while specific facilities directly at the Fuente de San Miguel recreational area might be limited, you can typically find cafes and restaurants within the town itself to enjoy before or after your hike.
